completed.title: Survey Completed!
completed.description: Thank you for completing the survey.

cover.title: Do you know your professional carbon footprint?
cover.subtitle: Get an estimate in just 5 minutes!

why.title: Why this tool?
why.description: [PROTOTYPE] This work-related carbon footprint tool is part of your organisation's transition approach by providing an order-of-magnitude estimate of greenhouse gas emissions linked to professional practices. It raises awareness by making the topic more tangible, while addressing emissions at employee level. In that sense, it connects individual actions with structural challenges and provides a useful entry point to better understand the main emission categories and foster collective thinking on reduction levers at organisation level.

about.title: About the development of this tool
about.description: This tool was developed by the Association pour la Transition Bas Carbone with committed partner organisations. This version is freely based on the official Nos Gestes Climat version developed by ADEME (French Agency for Ecological Transition) in partnership with ABC (Association pour la Transition Bas Carbone).

note.title: Note
note.description: This tool is currently a prototype: it may contain bugs, incomplete features, or some missing information and messages. These will be progressively fixed and improved.

interstitial.title: Some orders of magnitude for comparison:

completion.title: My work-related footprint
completion.headlineDescription: Your highest emission categories and the most effective actions to reduce them.

transition.title: Trajectory to {target} tCO₂e
transition.description: You start from {current} tCO₂e/year to move toward the {target} tCO₂e goal.

alone.question: Can I do it all on my own?
alone.answer: No. The ecological transition is a collective effort. The State, local authorities and companies all have a key role. Individual actions still matter to lead by example and accelerate change.

start.question: Where should I start?
start.answer: Focus on your two or three biggest emission sources first. High-impact actions usually reduce car travel, improve food habits, and avoid unnecessary digital purchases.

subject.newUser: You have been invited on My Impact Pro
subject.addedActiveUser: You have been invited on a new environment My Impact Pro
subject.activation: You have activated you account on My Impact Pro
subject.activationRequest: Access request to your My Impact Pro organization

guide.title: Campaign guide
guide.description: Each row corresponds to a survey campaign sent to your employees.
guide.resultsDescription: click the chart icon to open the campaign dashboard (average footprint, response rate, category breakdown).
guide.csvDescription: click the download icon to export responses as a CSV file and analyze them in your reporting tools.
guide.shareDescription: click the copy icon in "Link to share", then send the link to your employees (email, intranet, internal messaging).

role.ADMIN_description: An administrator manages My Impact Pro organization settings, campaigns, and team members.
role.COLLABORATOR_description: A collaborator can answer the survey and view results depending on assigned permissions.
role.Not authorized: You are not allowed to do this action
role.More than one: There must be at least one admin or manager in the organisation

results.title: Results Dashboard
results.subtitle: Anonymised results from the awareness campaign

objective.nationalTarget: National target: {value} t CO₂e per person by 2050
objective.nationalTargetDescription: France's goal to reach carbon neutrality by 2050 and help limit global warming to +2 °C.
objective.aboveTarget: Today you are above {target} tonnes, the intermediate target we propose for 2030.
objective.paceTitle: Your indicative pace to reach {target} T by {year}

commute.title: Commute
commute.carModeShare: Share of commute trips by car
commute.publicTransportModeShare: Share of commute trips by public transport
commute.activeModeShare: Share of commute trips by active mobility
commute.avgPublicTransportKm: Average distance travelled by public transport (commute)
commute.avgEmissionPerMode: Average carbon footprint from commute trips

transport.title: Transport
transport.carKm: Kilometres driven by car on average

travel.title: Business travel
travel.trainModeShare: Share of business trips by train
travel.carTravelModeShare: Share of business trips by car
travel.planeTravelModeShare: Share of business trips by plane
travel.avgTravelEmissionByMode: Average carbon footprint from business trips

food.title: Food
food.vegMealsShare: Share of consumed meals that are vegetarian
food.veganMealsShare: Share of consumed meals that are vegan
food.redMeatDailyEmployees: Employees who eat red meat every day

digital.title: Digital
digital.aiRequestsPerDay: Average AI requests per employee per day
digital.videoHoursPerDay: Average videoconference hours per employee per day
digital.internetHoursPerDay: Average internet hours per employee per day

collectiveEffort.title: Important: it is also a collective effort

state.label: The French State
state.description: It can accelerate the decarbonisation of society by passing laws and targeted financial support, as well as through its investments.

collectivities.label: Local authorities
collectivities.description: Through their land-use planning choices, they contribute to the adoption of more sustainable practices at a local level.

companies.label: Companies
companies.description: Under the impetus of the State and citizens, companies are working towards the progressive reduction of the footprint of goods and services.

equivalent.title: What does it correspond to?
equivalent.description: Compare the campaign's average footprint with concrete references to better understand its impact.
